variables.name: Leading-edge logic fabrication (EUV / sub-7nm)

actor_readings.reading: Binding chokepoint; the minimum stave for frontier-training independence, medium-high confidence (#4,#19).
Controller, not consumer-constrained; leverage held via ASML/EDA/TSMC coalition (#88,#90,#91).

variables.current_reading: Firmly allied-controlled at the frontier: no domestic EUV in China, SMIC capped ~7nm-equivalent via DUV multi-patterning (#4,#23,#1). Upstream EDA (#90) and ASML EUV (#1) remain single-source. High confidence the frontier gate holds to ~2028.
variables.trajectory: Slowest-moving variable; genuine indigenization is a 10–30 year tacit-process problem — turbine-blade metallurgy took China ~30yr for partial parity (#A34), silk/Murano monopolies held ~400yr and broke via single-defection not replication (#A31,#A32), ASML EUV assessed 15–25yr from China (#A33). BUT architectural substitution (DUV multipatterning, chiplets, custom ASICs) narrows the PRACTICAL gap far faster than true node replication (#23,#31). Clock: decade+ for parity, but 'good-enough for domestic AI' within ~5yr per Swiss-watch bifurcation (#A36).
variables.base_rate: Tacit-manufacturing monopolies durably hold roughly one crisis-cycle to several decades (jet engines 70+yr #A16; Haber-Bosch held one war #A29). Analogy BREAKS because the AI software layer (open weights) lets China get baseline capability without frontier fab — no prior tacit-monopoly had a free 'recipe' escape hatch.

variables.name: Algorithmic efficiency & open-weight diffusion

actor_readings.reading: Primary beneficiary; efficiency + open weights relieve chip denial (#3,#9,#79).

variables.current_reading: Baseline already irreversibly diffused; DeepSeek R1 matched GPT-4-class on export-compliant H800s at ~$6M compute (#3), and open weights (Llama/Mistral/Qwen/DeepSeek) transfer both efficiency AND de-risked R&D recipes (#2,#21). Effective gap compressing toward months (#11). Medium-high confidence.
variables.trajectory: Fastest-moving; irreversible within weeks-to-months once published (crypto controls collapsed ~8yr #A2; TPS/lean methods became global baseline #A50; TRIPS/generic-pharma #A17). Constraint may PARADOXICALLY accelerate Chinese efficiency (#9,#A54 Shahed, #A49 Zero). Clock: months.
variables.base_rate: Digitized dual-use knowledge diffuses within a decade, usually a few years (crypto ~8yr #A2; Stuxnet <5yr #A12). BREAKS: advancing the frontier still needs lumpy training compute — diffused weights ≠ ability to keep pace with the moving frontier.

variables.name: Power / energy & datacenter buildout capacity

actor_readings.reading: Binding minimum stave for its OWN buildout — grid/water, with no policy lever, medium confidence (#55,#62).
Non-binding / structural advantage once chips clear (#56).

variables.current_reading: Actor-split. US: grid interconnection queue >2,000GW with 5+yr waits (#55), water limits in NoVa/Phoenix (#62), nuclear restart 10+yr licensing (#57) — power is becoming binding by ~2028 (#54). China: ~150GW/yr buildout, co-located hydro/coal/nuclear via state industrial parks (#56,#57). Medium-high confidence power displaces chips as US bottleneck.
variables.trajectory: US grid slow and un-export-controllable; China fast and state-directed. Datacenter build is 18–36mo vs fab 5–7yr, so the effective control window = chip-sufficiency date minus datacenter lead-time (#63). Crossover ~2028 (#54).
variables.base_rate: No clean tech-race analogy; energy-denial historically triggers substitution (OPEC → North Sea/French nuclear #A14) but on decade timescales. There is NO export-control lever on electricity (#54).

variables.name: CUDA / integrated-ecosystem software moat

actor_readings.reading: Binds usability of indigenous chips — fab parity would not erase this software gap (#19).

variables.current_reading: 17-yr head start; Huawei CANN and others show 30–60% efficiency gaps on real workloads (#19); talent/capital/cloud aggregation compounds it (#8). RISC-V (#15) and Chinese stacks growing but sub-frontier. Medium confidence the moat holds near-term.
variables.trajectory: Very durable — coordination-equilibrium moats (Bloomberg 40yr #A55, x86 vs RISC ~30yr #A57, Adobe/SAP #A59,#A58) persist for decades and break only at a PLATFORM DISCONTINUITY, not head-to-head (e.g. if inference/edge displaces datacenter training — #5,#30). Clock: decade unless the paradigm shifts to inference-time compute.
variables.base_rate: Ecosystem/format moats last ~30yr and break at discontinuity, not on merit (#A57,#A55). BREAKS if inference-time scaling (#5) makes the datacenter-CUDA heritage irrelevant.

variables.name: Coalition compliance & enforcement ratchet

actor_readings.reading: Coalition manager; enforcement agent is also the commercial beneficiary of looser rules — regulatory-capture risk (#47).
Swing complier; sovereign-AI programs (Mistral) fragment from within (#51,#60).
Third-country valve — structural incentive to prevent bifurcation, transacts both stacks (#29,#61).

variables.current_reading: Currently in an unusual TIGHTENING phase: FDPR extraterritoriality (#88), Japan-NL trilateral outside Wassenaar (#91), Entity-List spillover cordon (#92), enforcement headcount/penalties rising (#94), informed-compliance private enforcement (#95), multi-dimensional threshold ratchet (#89,#97). Counter-pressure: internal lobbying and the NVIDIA revenue-capture trap (#46,#47), Wassenaar consensus paralysis (#26), Gulf/SEA hubs (#29). Contested, medium confidence.
variables.trajectory: COCOM base rate: multilateral controls erode over 15–30yr under commercial pressure and single-actor defection (#A1; Toshiba-Kongsberg one transaction closed the sub-propeller gap #A25). Currently defying that arc via FDPR coercion, but China's market gravity grows. Clock: years-to-decade.
variables.base_rate: Multilateral tech-denial coalitions erode 15–30yr (#A1); a single commercially-motivated defection can be decisive (#A25). BREAKS: FDPR gives the US coercive extraterritorial reach COCOM never had (#88).

variables.name: Circumvention bandwidth (smuggling + legal arbitrage + cloud API)

actor_readings.reading: Beneficiary — delay not denial; inference via API/open weights already largely irreversible (#42,#53).
Enforcement conflicted by hyperscaler revenue; KYC verification gap structural (#44,#50).

variables.current_reading: Porous. Transshipment via SG/MY/UAE (#7); black-market H100 premiums ($40–70k) confirm controls bite AND smuggling is rational (#28); prior legal A800/H800 arbitrage (#24); static thresholds decaying with perf/watt (#59); permanent rulemaking-vs-adaptation lag (#58). The cloud-API / hosted-inference leg is essentially ungoverned (#42,#43,#44,#53) — a permanent bypass for inference. Medium confidence 'porous-delay' for hardware, 'wide-open' for API.
variables.trajectory: Hardware vectors are being closed by the multi-dimensional ratchet (#89,#97), raising the circumvention floor. But service-layer delivery structurally defeats goods-controls (VoIP #A48, Eurodollar #A43, satellite imagery #A46). Cloud/API and overseas hyperscaler datacenters (#61) are the un-sealable frontier.
variables.base_rate: Goods-controls fail categorically once capability is delivered as an internet service (#A48); hardware embargoes leak 20–40% (#7). BREAKS: frontier TRAINING is lumpy/physical and harder to deliver as a service than voice or dollars.

variables.name: Transformative-AI decisive window (timing vs diffusion clock)
variables.current_reading: Genuinely unresolved — this is the meta-crux (#65). If a recursive-self-improvement / strategic-discontinuity threshold arrives 2026–2032, controls need hold only ~6–8yr, which fab timelines plausibly deliver; if 2035+, the diffusion critiques become decisive (#65,#39,#66,#11,#64). Low confidence on timing.
variables.trajectory: Non-linear: a lead held THROUGH an RSI threshold compounds endogenously into unbridgeable advantage (#39,#66), inverting the usual linear catch-up. This reframes the goal from 'large lead' to 'reach threshold first'.
variables.base_rate: NO historical analogy — every prior tech race (COCOM, crypto, nuclear, aviation) was linear and catch-up-able within 8–30yr. This is precisely where the entire analogy set breaks; RSI would make lead-preservation (#64) a coherent success criterion for the first time.

variables.name: Taiwan / TSMC supply continuity

actor_readings.reading: Recovery path opens only if CHIPS-Act diversification precedes contingency (#37,#40).
Symmetrically hostage — cannot destroy TSMC without gutting its own frontier supply (#36).

variables.current_reading: Deterrence holding. MAPD symmetry: destroying TSMC catastrophically sets back China's OWN AI trajectory (#36); scorched-earth protocols + ASML remote kill-switch make destruction/degradation far more likely than intact capture (#34,#35). Stockpiles buffer 18–36mo of training compute (#40). Low near-term disruption probability, high tail severity.
variables.trajectory: Diversification (TSMC Arizona N2, Intel 18A) reaches scale ~2028–2030 (#37) — a trackable race against any contingency window. Post-destruction, China's 28nm inference fleet may outlast Western leading-edge loss (8–12yr rebuild) (#41).
variables.base_rate: Infrastructure denial is decisive at the conflict moment but transient — the British WWI cable-cut forced adversary onto inferior substitutes then drove parallel build-out (#A28). Mutual dependence makes this a hostage/MAD structure, not a one-sided lever.

variables.name: Global South standards-war / adoption contest

actor_readings.reading: Ahead via financing + open-weight cadence; a separable success condition independent of frontier parity (#76,#79).
Reactive; frontier-lead policy is largely orthogonal to this contest (#77).

variables.current_reading: Contested and trending China across ~100 developing nations: Huawei-5G template (#78), Digital Silk Road concessional financing Western firms can't match (#80), open-weight 'dumping' for market capture (#79), local-infra/data-sovereignty edge (#86), language-capability advantage (#85). Western counter (AWS/Google/MSFT subsidized access #83) is smaller-scale. Medium confidence China-favored here.
variables.trajectory: Fast lock-in: 3–5yr procurement decisions create 10–15yr path dependencies (toolchains, ITU-T standards citations, developer labor markets) (#81,#82,#87). Largely DECOUPLED from the frontier — 'good-enough' DUV-era models satisfy the decisive use-cases (#77,#14).
variables.base_rate: Huawei 5G captured ~40% share / ~100 nations before Western response (#78,#A6). Payment-network pattern: state-backed challenger wins domestic/aligned markets, Western incumbent holds cross-border/frontier (#A56).

variables.name: Talent / human-capital diffusion

actor_readings.reading: Net beneficiary via returnees + MCF, partially offset by international-collaboration chill (#25,#16,#27).

variables.current_reading: Diffusing toward China. Visa/OPT friction reduces US inflow AND pushes experienced researchers home (#25); MCF mandates commercial-to-military transfer (#16); academic pipeline transfers tacit knowledge no weight file contains (#17). Medium confidence on direction.
variables.trajectory: Historically the SHARPEST control-failure mode — knowledge walks across borders: Paperclip/Osoaviakhim nullified Germany's rocket lead in months (#A24), Khan network (#A4), British textile workers (#A7), silk monopoly broken by smuggled expertise (#A31). Continuous, uncontrollable clock.
variables.base_rate: Human-capital transfer defeats hardware controls repeatedly (#A24,#A4,#A7); mobile trainers make chip controls 'incomplete'. BREAKS partially: MCF also creates innovation drag / VC-chilling offset (#27).

edges.note: TSMC continuity gates ALL parties' leading-edge supply; a Taiwan discontinuity resets V1 to near-zero for everyone (#36,#37).
US/allied control of ASML EUV, EDA tools and the trilateral equipment regime is the upstream chokepoint holding China's fab gate shut (#88,#90,#91).
CUDA/ecosystem lock means even indigenous or captured chips underperform 30–60% — software gate persists even at fab parity (#19,#8).
China must clear chip-sufficiency before its structural power/datacenter advantage becomes the governing constraint (#56,#63).
Algorithmic efficiency (DeepSeek MoE/distillation) offsets 1–2 chip generations, relieving the fab chokepoint (#3,#9).
Smuggling, legal downgraded-chip arbitrage, and cloud-API inference relieve chip denial — converting 'denial' into 'delay' (#7,#24,#42).
Coalition compliance and enforcement capacity set the leakage bandwidth; carve-outs or Gulf/SEA hubs widen it (#13,#29,#94).
The decisive-window timing determines whether fast diffusion matters at all: pre-2032 TAI makes ~6–8yr of controls sufficient; 2035+ lets diffusion win (#65,#64).
Mobile talent transfers tacit R&D certainty independent of open weights or chips — an additive escape vector (#17,#21).
'Good-enough' open-weight models are the enabler of China's Global South capture; frontier parity is not required (#77,#79).
Both are candidate binding physical inputs; whichever binds first governs — chips bind China, power binds the US, and the binding constraint may shift by ~2028 (#54).
Global-South toolchain/standards adoption (MindSpore, ITU-T citations) compounds ecosystem lock-in, extending or eroding the CUDA moat by region (#81,#82).

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will China (SMIC/Huawei) volume-produce a sub-5nm-class logic node at commercial yield by 2028-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Credible teardown/industry report (TechInsights or equivalent) confirming a commercially shipped Chinese <5nm-class logic product at reported yields >50% before 31 Dec 2028.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a China-trained model rank within the top 3 globally (or within 3 months of the frontier) on a major public benchmark leaderboard by 2026-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A model from a China-based lab placed top-3 on a recognized leaderboard (e.g. LMArena/Chatbot Arena or a standard reasoning benchmark) as of 31 Dec 2026.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will US grid interconnection remain a publicly-cited binding constraint (multi-year delays denying/deferring datacenter loads) through 2027-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: FERC/utility/major-hyperscaler public statements citing >12-month interconnection delays materially limiting AI datacenter buildout in at least one major US market as of 31 Dec 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the Netherlands AND Japan maintain semiconductor-equipment controls without a major China carve-out through 2027-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: No publicly announced material relaxation or China-specific exemption to Dutch (ASML) or Japanese equipment export controls between now and 31 Dec 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will the US implement enforceable KYC/cloud-API export controls covering frontier hosted-model access for foreign users by 2027-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A BIS/Treasury final rule in force by 31 Dec 2027 imposing mandatory end-user verification on US cloud/API access to frontier models, beyond proposed/draft status.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a kinetic or blockade event materially disrupting TSMC leading-edge output occur before 2028-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A publicly confirmed military action (blockade, strike, or invasion) causing a >1-month halt to TSMC leading-edge production before 31 Dec 2028.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a credible recursive-self-improvement / transformative-AI threshold be widely reported as crossed by 2030-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Consensus among two or more major AI labs plus a government AI-safety body that an AI system is materially and autonomously accelerating frontier AI R&D, reported before 31 Dec 2030.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a Chinese-origin AI stack (model or cloud) be the default in a majority of new government AI deployments across surveyed Belt-and-Road / Global South states by 2028-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: A credible multi-country survey/report finding Chinese-origin AI infrastructure as the primary vendor in >50% of new government AI procurements across a representative Global South sample by 31 Dec 2028.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will a non-CUDA stack (Huawei CANN/Ascend or equivalent) exceed 20% of China's domestic AI training-compute share by 2027-12-31?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Industry analyst estimate (e.g. SemiAnalysis/Omdia) putting non-NVIDIA/non-CUDA accelerators at >20% of China domestic training compute deployed as of 31 Dec 2027.

forecast_signals.binary_question: Will Huawei Ascend (or Chinese peers combined) ship >1 million advanced AI accelerators for domestic use in a single year by 2027?
forecast_signals.resolution_criteria: Credible reporting of >1M advanced Chinese AI accelerators shipped/produced for domestic deployment in calendar-year 2026 or 2027.

binding_constraint_by_actor.reading: Minimum stave = leading-edge chip supply (V1), further gated by the CUDA/ecosystem moat (V4) and upstream allied EDA/ASML control (V5). But this slow-clock constraint is materially SOFTENED by three fast vectors — efficiency/open-weight diffusion (V2), circumvention incl. cloud-API (V6) and mobile talent (V10) — so the binding constraint is real for FRONTIER TRAINING yet not for baseline/deployment capability (#4,#19,#3,#42,#A24). Medium confidence.

Minimum stave = domestic power/grid + water (V3), a constraint with NO export-control lever, compounded by coalition-durability management (V5) where its own enforcement agent (NVIDIA) is the commercial beneficiary of looser rules (#55,#62,#47). The US frontier-lead is otherwise strong; its binding constraints are self-inflicted/internal. Medium confidence.

Minimum stave = dependence within the allied stack + weak sovereign frontier capability (V5); its sovereign-AI programs (Mistral) both hedge that dependence and fragment the coalition, while EU AI Act compliance adds self-handicapping drag (#51,#60). Low-medium confidence.

Minimum stave = compliance positioning (V5/V6) — structurally incentivized to transact both stacks and prevent bifurcation, making it the pivotal third-country valve and datacenter-sovereignty host rather than a capability-constrained actor (#29,#61). Medium confidence.

summary: The graph shows a race between two clocks, not a single winner. China's frontier ambitions are gated by a SLOW-clock chokepoint stack — leading-edge fab (V1), which is itself gated by the CUDA/ecosystem moat (V4) and upstream by allied EDA/ASML control (V5) — where tacit-knowledge analogies imply a decade-plus catch-up (#A31,#A33,#A34). But that gate is simultaneously eroded by three FAST-clock vectors: algorithmic efficiency / open-weight diffusion (V2), circumvention bandwidth including the ungoverned cloud-API leg (V6), and mobile talent (V10) — each measured in months-to-years and largely uncontrollable (#3,#42,#A2,#A24). The US's own binding constraint is not a chokepoint it holds but one it cannot export-control at all: domestic power/grid and water (V3), which bind the Western buildout while China's state-directed energy scales freely (#55,#56,#62). Taiwan (V8) is a mutual hostage — a Mutual-Assured-Production-Destruction node that can zero BOTH sides' leading-edge supply (#36). The decisive meta-variable is timing (V7): if a transformative/RSI threshold lands in 2026–2032 the controls only need to hold ~6–8 years and lead-preservation is coherent; if it is 2035+, the diffusion vectors win. Separately, the Global South standards contest (V9) is trending China's way on a 3–5 year lock-in clock regardless of who holds the frontier (#77,#87). Honest read: contested and clock-dependent — security-denial at the frontier can succeed while the paradigm still "fails" on diffusion, commercial capture, and the Global South simultaneously (#49).
